channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Refrigeration down to 0.16 K using a frustrated magnet Gd<sub>2</sub>B<sub>2</sub>MoO<sub>9</sub>
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 07 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-68278-z

Ultra-low-temperature magnetic refrigeration is strongly dependent on the performances of refrigerants. Here the authors provide a frustrated magnet Gd2B2MoO9 with excellent magnetocaloric performances and a minimum temperature of 0.16 K is achieved.
item.dc:title: Refrigeration down to 0.16 K using a frustrated magnet Gd<sub>2</sub>B<sub>2</sub>MoO<sub>9</sub>
item.dc:creator: Yikun Zhang
Yingzhe Na
Xinyang Liu
Junsen Xiang
Fengying Chen
Hai-Feng Li
Peijie Sun
Shengqiang Zhou
Xuefeng Zhang
Lingwei Li

item.title: Long-distance spin transport in frustrated hyperkagome magnet Gd<sub>3</sub>Ga<sub>5</sub>O<sub>12</sub>
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 07 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-68203-4

Spin transport properties of magnetically ordered materials have been well studied. Here, the authors report an anomalous spin signal exhibiting spin transport over 480 microns in the frustrated hyperkagome magnetic insulator Gd3Ga5O12.
item.dc:title: Long-distance spin transport in frustrated hyperkagome magnet Gd<sub>3</sub>Ga<sub>5</sub>O<sub>12</sub>
item.dc:creator: Di Chen
Bingcheng Luo
Lei Xu
Zian Xia
Linhao Jia
Shaomian Qi
Congkuan Tian
Kangyao Chen
Hang Cui
Guangyi Chen
Shili Yan
Miaoling Huang
Jian Cui
Ya Feng
Zhentao Wang
Jiang Xiao
Jianhua Zhang
Ryuichi Shindou
X. C. Xie
Jian-Hao Chen

item.title: Reconfigurable all-solid-state topological lasing at arbitrary sites
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 06 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-68173-7

Topological lasers usually emit light from a fixed spot. Here, authors report on an all-solid-state platform where topological lasing is demonstrated at trivial lattice terminations as well as at reconfigurable arbitrary sites. The device consists of a Su-Schrieffer-Heeger waveguide array, printed in a disordered Nd:BaLaGa₃O₇ crystal.
item.dc:title: Reconfigurable all-solid-state topological lasing at arbitrary sites
item.dc:creator: Bo Wu
Jinting Ding
Wenchao Yan
Yuechen Jia
Haohai Yu
Xiang Ni
Feng Chen
